summary: Neutrophil infiltration occurs as an initial part of the body’s inflammatory response immediately after tissue damage; this makes it the earliest observable cellular change indicative of acute injury. Agent2 concluded that based on established biological mechanisms, A was the most accurate representation among given options for identifying such a primary event during wound healing or trauma-induced processes.
